---
title: "workflow_outline"
format: html
---

# Overall aims
Develop a mechanistic model of human-human infectious disease transmission that will be largely disease agnostic but as specificity builds will resemble Ebola virus disease.

#

```{r}
set.seed(20260604)
individual_index <- 1:10


population_states <- c(TRUE, rep(FALSE, 9))

```


Daily universal probabilities
```{r}
# probability_of_infection_per_contact if contact is infected
p_infection <- 0.1

# number of contacts each day for each individual
n_contacts <- 2
```

```{r}
# For testing
index <- 3

infection_function <- function(
    index,
    prob_infection,
    n_contacts,
    population_states
  ){
  state <- population_states[index]
  
  if(state) {
    return(TRUE)
  } else {
    contacts <- sample(
      x = population_states[-index],
      size = n_contacts,
      replace = FALSE
    )
    
    possible_infections <- prob_infection*contacts
    
    outcome <- rbinom(
      n = length(possible_infections),
      size = 1,
      prob = possible_infections
    )
    
    return(as.logical(max(outcome)))
    
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Time-steps

time_steps <- 10
population_over_time <- matrix(nrow = time_steps, ncol = length(population_states))
population_over_time[1,] <- population_states

for (t in 1:(time_steps-1)) {
  
  for (i in individual_index) {
    
    y <- infection_function(i, 0.1, 2, population_over_time[t,])
    population_over_time[t+1,i] <- y
  }
}

print(population_over_time)


```
